- ACTA-1: Slack thread replies now reach the agent. Previously the webhook only routed DMs and explicit `app_mention` events, so any follow-up message inside an existing HAL thread (`stop`, plain questions, etc.) was silently dropped. The router now also handles `message` events with a `thread_ts` when a conversation already exists for `${channelId}:${thread_ts}` — unrelated thread chatter is ignored because no conversation record matches. **Requires Slack app config update**: subscribe the bot to `message.channels` and `message.groups` bot events, and add `channels:history` / `groups:history` scopes.

- ACTA-12: Runs page grouped view loads faster — dropped `GROUPED_CUTOFF_DAYS` from 90 to 14 in `lambdas/admin-api/index.mjs`. The grouped handler still fetches the full cutoff window across all 7 trigger types on every page load with no pagination, so the window size directly drives latency. 14 days keeps normal recent activity visible while cutting the DynamoDB query volume ~6×. Follow-up: cursor-paginate `getGroupedRuns` to remove the window scan entirely.

- ACTA-13: Per-ticket run serialization via AWS Batch `dependsOn`. Previously two fix runs for the same ticket could be submitted in parallel (observed on PX-3184, two `pr-fix` runs 1m17s apart) because `hasActiveRunOfType` + `acquireLock` had a TOCTOU race — the 300s lock TTL only catches near-simultaneous redeliveries, not legitimately distinct triggers (check-suite-completed + pull-request-review) arriving >30s apart while the first fix is still RUNNING. Mention-on-mention still appends to pending list (preserved as a UX optimisation); mention-on-fix now chains instead of queuing onto the fix run.
